note: Crafts of The Unmade/The Making (destruction_creation axis). RUINWORK is the destruction-pole craft of the Unmakers — who hold that ending a thing WELL is a craft, and that the world is choked with what should have ended; MAKECRAFT is the creation-pole craft of the Wrights of the New, who cannot stop making and whose cities are never finished, which is the point. Neither is villainous: an Unmaker's clearing is a mercy and a Wright's ceaselessness is a waste. Authored by Aevi, SNG-054.

abilities.name: Ruinwork
abilities.narrationHints: It comes down the way he said it would, exactly where he said, and nothing else is touched.
abilities.description: The destruction-pole mastery: ending things well. Not violence — DEMOLITION, with an ethic: what should end, ends cleanly, and what should stand is left standing.
abilities.notFor: Ending what should stand (the Unmakers' whole discipline is that line); making; anything that requires patience with a dead thing.

tree.name: The Fault Line
tree.grants: See exactly where a thing wants to break — a wall, a plan, an alliance, an argument.
tree.cannot: Seeing the fault is not being right that it should be broken.

tree.name: The Clean Break
tree.grants: Break precisely at the fault — the thing comes apart where you chose and nowhere else.
tree.cannot: Precise is not reversible. A clean break is still a break.

tree.name: The Good Ending
tree.grants: End a thing WELL — a building, an institution, a life's work, a war — so that what it was is honored and what comes after is not poisoned by how it went.
tree.cannot: You must be right that it should end. The Unmakers' cult, the Scouring, is what this becomes when that judgment stops being made.

abilities.name: Fault-Sense
abilities.narrationHints: He looks at the thing and sees where it is already halfway to failing.
abilities.description: See where a thing is failing, or wants to — the first gift of the Unmade.
abilities.notFor: Seeing what should be built (Maker's-Eye); mending.

tree.name: Fault-Sense
tree.grants: See where a structure, plan, or relationship is under stress and where it will give.
tree.cannot: Seeing the fault does not tell you whether to press it.

tree.name: The Rotten Beam
tree.grants: Find the ONE piece whose failure brings the rest — the keystone of the collapse.
tree.cannot: Finding it makes you responsible for knowing it. You cannot un-know where the keystone is.

tree.name: The Whole Failing
tree.grants: Read the entire arc of a thing's decline — how it will end, in what order, and how long it has.
tree.cannot: You will be right, and nobody will want to hear it, and it will end exactly as you said.

abilities.name: Makecraft
abilities.narrationHints: It was not there this morning. It is there now, and it is not finished, and it is beautiful.
abilities.description: The creation-pole mastery: making, at speed and at scale. The Wrights raise things faster than anyone should be able to and never quite complete them.
abilities.notFor: Finishing (the Wrights are constitutionally bad at it); unmaking; making a thing that will not want to keep growing.

tree.name: The Raised Thing
tree.grants: Make something real, quickly — a bridge, a shelter, a tool, a wall — out of what is to hand.
tree.cannot: Quick is not sound. Raised things want tending, and the Wrights are already elsewhere.

tree.name: The Made Better
tree.grants: Make a thing better than it was — improve in the making, so what you raise exceeds what stood.
tree.cannot: Better by whose measure? The Wrights improve what nobody asked to have improved, constantly.

tree.name: The Unfinished
tree.grants: Deliberately leave a made thing incomplete — and it KEEPS MAKING ITSELF, growing on, adapting, never done.
tree.cannot: It never stops. The Forge-Eternal is what a whole city of this becomes, and the Wrights are genuinely unsure whether it is a triumph.

abilities.name: Maker's-Eye
abilities.narrationHints: She looks at the pile of junk and sees the thing it is about to be.
abilities.description: See what a thing could become — the first gift of the Making.
abilities.notFor: Seeing what a thing IS (Stone-Read); seeing where it fails (Fault-Sense). This sees what it COULD be.

tree.name: Maker's-Eye
tree.grants: See what could be made from what is here — materials, ruins, a half-broken thing.
tree.cannot: Seeing what could be made is not knowing whether it should be.

tree.name: The Better Shape
tree.grants: See the better form a thing is capable of, and the shortest road to it.
tree.cannot: There is always a better shape. Wrights at this rank cannot leave anything alone.

tree.name: The Thing It Wants to Be
tree.grants: See what a thing, a place, or a person is REACHING toward — the shape it would take if nothing stopped it.
tree.cannot: Seeing what someone could be is the most seductive and most dangerous sight in this Reach. It is not your business to build them.

abilities.name: The Raised Thing
abilities.narrationHints: It was not there. Now it is, and it will hold.
abilities.description: The Wright's first gift, opened to anyone who makes the journey: raise something real, quickly, from what is to hand. A bridge. A shelter. A tool. A barricade. The Wrights teach this one freely — more making in the world is the world they want, and this is the door.
abilities.notFor: Making things that will not want to keep growing; finishing (the craft carries the Wright's restlessness — what you raise will want tending, and you will want to move on).

tree.name: The Raised Thing
tree.grants: Make something real and functional, quickly — a bridge, a shelter, a tool, a wall — from what is here. It will hold long enough to matter.
tree.cannot: Quick is not sound. Raised things want tending, and the Wright in you is already somewhere else.

tree.name: Sound Work
tree.grants: What you make holds under pressure — weather, weight, time, a fight. Others would not have to repair it.
tree.cannot: Sound is not finished. The restlessness is not a flaw the Wrights want trained out of you.

tree.name: The Thing That Outlasts
tree.grants: Make something that will be here long after you. It will be found and used by people who do not know your name, and that will be enough.
tree.cannot: You will not see what becomes of it. The Wrights have made peace with this.

abilities.name: The Weapon at Hand
abilities.narrationHints: She looked at what was there and it was already a weapon. It just needed making.
abilities.description: Build a functional weapon from whatever is to hand — quickly, correctly, with enough craft that it will do what it needs to.

tree.name: Improvised
tree.grants: Quickly make a functional weapon from available materials — one that will not break, will land, and will hurt.

tree.name: Made to Harm
tree.grants: Build a weapon fast enough to matter in a fight and good enough to be dangerous — not a masterwork, but the right thing for what you're facing right now.

tree.name: The Right Tool
tree.grants: Look at the materials at hand and see the weapon they are already; build it in moments, and it is exactly what this situation required.

abilities.name: The Work Speaks
abilities.narrationHints: She didn't say anything. She showed them what she'd built. The argument was over.
abilities.description: Let the made thing speak. The quality of your work commands a kind of authority no argument reaches.

tree.name: Demonstrated Craft
tree.grants: Show what you have made — or make something now — and let its quality stand as argument; the person who would have dismissed you reconsiders.

tree.name: The Reputation
tree.grants: Your work is known enough that naming yourself — or showing it — commands genuine respect and opens doors that stay closed to those who only claim.

tree.name: Undeniable
tree.grants: The thing you have built is the argument and the evidence. Anyone who has seen it cannot pretend otherwise — and they have all seen it.

abilities.name: The Built Way
abilities.narrationHints: There was no crossing. Now there is. She built it.
abilities.description: Build a path where none existed — bridge a gap, span a drop, cut a stair into a cliff — fast enough to matter.

tree.name: The Crossing
tree.grants: Build a passage across an obstacle — a bridge, a stair, a ramp — quickly enough that the party can use it before circumstances change.

tree.name: Made Passage
tree.grants: Open a way through or across any natural or built obstacle by constructing what's needed — not by force, by craft.

tree.name: The Road That Wasn't
tree.grants: Build a path where there was truly none — spanning what shouldn't be spanned, opening what was sealed — and have it hold the weight it needs to hold.

abilities.name: The Ongoing Work
abilities.narrationHints: Keep the hands moving and the self keeps going.
abilities.description: Sustain through making — the wright whose hands are busy doesn't run out; the work is what holds them together.

tree.name: Hands Moving
tree.grants: As long as you are making something, fear, grief, exhaustion, and cold have less purchase — the work is the anchor that keeps you functional.

tree.name: Built Through It
tree.grants: Sustain yourself and your party through hardship by making useful things — shelter, tools, warmth, structure — that make the hardship survivable.

tree.name: Left in Motion
tree.grants: Start something building and leave it — the structure grows, the mechanism runs, the work continues. What you set in motion sustains beyond your direct attention.

abilities.name: The Clean Removal
abilities.narrationHints: Remove what should not be there and what should be there can exist again.
abilities.description: Restore by precise subtraction — not by adding what's missing but by ending what's corrupting, blocking, or consuming the thing that needs to heal.

tree.name: Precise Removal
tree.grants: Identify and remove exactly what is interfering with a person or system's recovery — the splinter, the bad element, the thing that was keeping it from healing on its own.

tree.name: What Shouldn't Be There
tree.grants: Restore function by ending the specific thing that was consuming it — corruption, disease, obstruction — with precision that doesn't damage what should remain.

tree.name: Clean
tree.grants: Remove everything that should not be there, completely and correctly, so that what remains can be what it was meant to be without interference.

abilities.name: The Ended Threat
abilities.narrationHints: He saw where it was going. He ended it before it arrived.
abilities.description: Protect by ending the threat before it can act — not blocking but prevention through precise, early intervention.

tree.name: Early Intervention
tree.grants: See a threat forming and end it at the source — the plan that collapses before it executes, the attack that never completes because you dismantled its condition.

tree.name: The Stopped Thing
tree.grants: Protect someone by finding and breaking the specific thing that would harm them — not deflecting the blow but ending the arm that swings it.

tree.name: Before It Arrives
tree.grants: Identify a danger in its earliest form and end it completely, so it never becomes the threat it was becoming.

abilities.name: The Open Material
abilities.narrationHints: Break it back to what it was before it was this, and it can be something else.
abilities.description: Unmake the current form of a thing so completely that what remains is raw material — ready to become something else.

tree.name: Back to Parts
tree.grants: Disassemble a made thing into its components without destroying the components — what was one thing becomes usable pieces of something else.

tree.name: Unformed
tree.grants: Break a thing down to the point where it has no remaining identity as what it was — it is now material, and material can become anything.

tree.name: The Open Form
tree.grants: Return something complex — a structure, a plan, a fixed belief — to the state before it became that specific thing, clearing the way for something new to take form.

abilities.name: The Demonstrated End
abilities.narrationHints: They knew what he could end. They took his word seriously.
abilities.description: The authority of someone who has shown they can end things correctly — their word carries because everyone knows what they can do.

tree.name: The Known Capacity
tree.grants: Your reputation for ending things precisely and completely gives weight to what you say — a warning from you lands differently than from someone without your history.

tree.name: The Demonstrated Threat
tree.grants: Show what you can dismantle — or name it plainly — and command the terms of an agreement; they know what refusal means.

tree.name: The Final Authority
tree.grants: You are the person who decides what ends. In a situation where endings are at stake, your word is the one that stands — not because you outranked anyone, but because everyone knows you can do what you say.

abilities.name: The Ended Burden
abilities.narrationHints: End the thing that's consuming you. Then go on.
abilities.description: Sustain by precisely ending what's wearing you down — the habit, the wound, the relationship, the weight — rather than trying to carry it further.

tree.name: The Cut
tree.grants: Identify precisely what is consuming your capacity — exhaustion, fear, a specific obligation — and end it cleanly so you can continue.

tree.name: What You Don't Need
tree.grants: Sustain yourself by releasing everything non-essential across a hard stretch — not forcing through with more, but needing less through precise subtraction.

tree.name: The Lighter Load
tree.grants: End what is consuming your group — despair, a failed plan, an unsustainable commitment — correctly enough that what remains can actually go on.
